one_line_takeaway: Releasing counterfactual explanations alongside predictions in MLaaS systems substantially amplifies shadow-based membership inference success -- even under differential privacy -- and DP+Active-Learning defenses only partially and unevenly mitigate this at the level of actual training-member protection (micro-level), while leaving CF quality largely intact.
key_finding: Baseline (no privacy defense) achieves the highest predictive performance (96% accuracy/precision/recall on EEG; 100% on In-Location); Only-AL matches this using a reduced subset (77.45% of EEG data, only 10% of In-Location data). Adding DP degrades utility especially at low epsilon (e.g., EEG Only-DP accuracy 82% at eps=1 rising to 90% at eps=10; In-Location Only-DP drops as low as 40% accuracy at eps=1). Critically, exposing CFs substantially strengthens MIA across every defense configuration: on EEG, Baseline (No-CF) attains 58% accuracy/recall vs. 77% with CFs; Only-DP (No-CF vs CF) accuracy/recall rises from 26%->43% (eps=1), 27%->79% (eps=3), 38%->74% (eps=5), 57%->68% (eps=10); on In-Location, Only-DP (No-CF vs CF) recall rises from 37/44/61/65% to 48/70/74/70% across eps=1,3,5,10. At the micro level (fraction of true training members whose membership is hidden), CFs sharply erode protection even when DP is applied: on EEG under DP-Post-AL, micro-defended ratio drops from 68% to 44% at eps=5 and from 56% to 9% at eps=10 when CFs are exposed; under AL-Guided-DP it drops from 54% to 14% at eps=3. Macro-level defended ratios decline less sharply because they also count never-trained records, so aggregate exposure metrics can mask this individual-level vulnerability (e.g., EEG AL-Guided-DP macro drops from 65.74% to 35.95% at eps=3 while micro protection is far worse). By contrast, DP has only a limited effect on CF quality itself: EEG proximity stays roughly stable (5.60-7.15 across DP variants) and sparsity is only mildly reduced under tight budgets (eps=1), with In-Location showing similar patterns modulated by its higher dimensionality.
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution (not related work) is threefold: (1) it systematically quantifies how counterfactual explanations (CFs) can be used as an adversary's knowledge source to strengthen shadow-based membership inference attacks (MIAs) against MLaaS APIs, comparing a 'No-CF' shadow dataset (model posteriors only) against a 'CF-enabled' shadow dataset (posteriors + CF explanation E(x)); (2) it proposes a novel defense framework that jointly combines Differential Privacy (DP-SGD) with Active Learning (AL) as a data-distillation strategy, evaluated in five training configurations -- Baseline, Only-AL, Only-DP, DP-Post-AL (AL first, then DP), and AL-Guided-DP (DP applied at each AL iteration); and (3) it empirically characterizes the resulting three-way trade-off among MIA privacy leakage, model predictive performance, and CF quality (proximity/sparsity), introducing two new evaluation metrics -- micro-level and macro-level 'defended record ratio' -- to separate protection of actual training members from aggregate dataset exposure.
threat_model: Attacker has black-box API query access to an MLaaS-deployed DNN classifier f_theta and, in the CF-enabled setting, also to a counterfactual explainer E that the model owner exposes alongside predictions. The attacker builds a shadow dataset by repeatedly querying the API (No-CF: {f_theta(x_i)}; CF-enabled: {E(x_i), f_theta(x_i)}), trains shadow DNN models to approximate the target model's behavior, and trains an XGBoost attack model g(p_s(x) || phi(E(x),x)) on shadow-model outputs (both member and non-member samples, since the attacker fully controls shadow dataset membership) to predict whether a queried point was in the target's training set D. This is a classic shadow-model membership inference threat model (Shokri et al. 2017) extended so the adversary's side-channel includes CF explanation content in addition to prediction posteriors.
limitations: The paper explicitly excludes the CF-distance-threshold MIA baseline (Pawelczyk et al.) from comparison, stating its performance depends strongly on the chosen CF generator and feature scaling/normalization, making fair reproduction and threshold calibration ambiguous -- so the shadow-based attack is not benchmarked against this alternative attack family. Evaluation is limited to two tabular datasets (EEG, In-Location) and a single CF method (NICE); generalization to other explanation types (feature-attribution, SHAP/LIME) or to image/text domains is not tested. AL-Guided-DP and DP-Post-AL sometimes require retaining larger training subsets than Only-AL to reach comparable performance, which the authors note works against the privacy-reduction rationale for using AL. The authors also note that practical usability/interpretability of CFs under DP for real end users was not evaluated empirically (they flag this as future work requiring user studies).

datasets_used: EEG Eye State (UCI): 14,980 records, 14 numerical features, 2 classes
UJIIndoorLoc / In-Location: 20,000 records, 529 Wi-Fi features, multi-building/multi-floor labels
quantitative_results: EEG Only-AL matches Baseline performance using 77.45% of training data
In-Location Only-AL matches Baseline performance using only 10% of training data
EEG Baseline MIA accuracy/recall: 58% (No-CF) vs 77% (CF); precision 70% (No-CF) vs 71% (CF)
EEG Only-AL MIA accuracy/recall: 50% (No-CF) vs 72% (CF); precision 64-65%
EEG Only-DP MIA accuracy/recall (No-CF -> CF) by eps: 26%->43% (eps=1), 27%->79% (eps=3), 38%->74% (eps=5), 57%->68% (eps=10); precision ~67-69% roughly stable
In-Location Only-DP MIA recall (No-CF -> CF) by eps: 37->48% (eps=1), 44->70% (eps=3), 61->74% (eps=5), 65->70% (eps=10)
In-Location DP-Post-AL MIA accuracy (No-CF -> CF) by eps=1,3,10: 43->63%, 38->52%, 52->71% (eps=5 was an exception to the trend)
EEG DP-Post-AL micro-defended record ratio: 68%->44% at eps=5 and 56%->9% at eps=10 when CFs are exposed
EEG AL-Guided-DP micro-defended record ratio: 54%->14% at eps=3 when CFs are exposed
EEG AL-Guided-DP macro-defended record ratio at eps=3: 65.74%->35.95% when CFs are exposed
EEG CF proximity range across DP variants: Only-DP 5.78-6.22; DP-Post-AL 5.60-7.15 (largely stable under DP)
AL subset sizes: EEG AL-Guided-DP selects 78.97%, 74.48%, 56.49%, 66.98% of data for eps=1,3,5,10; In-Location DP-Post-AL requires 29.13%, 21%, 40.35% of data for eps=1,3,5
baselines_compared: Only-AL (active learning data selection, no DP)
DP-Post-AL (AL subset selection first, DP-SGD applied afterward)
AL-Guided-DP (paper's proposed joint approach: DP applied within each AL iteration)
